title: Justice Department Ends Investigation of Fed Chair
summary: The Justice Department has stopped looking into whether Jerome Powell, the leader of the Federal Reserve, lied about repairs at his office. Instead, another group will check on the costs. This news is important because it means a new person, Kevin Warsh, might become the next Fed chair. The White House wants the Senate to approve him quickly. Also, a court said President Trump cannot stop people from asking for asylum at the southern border. The court said the law allows people to seek safety. The government can ask a higher court to change this decision. Lastly, the FDA is going to speed up reviews of three new medicines for mental health conditions.

keywords.explanation: The part of the U.S. government that enforces laws and investigates crimes.
The central bank of the United States that manages money and interest rates.
Protection given by a country to people who are fleeing danger in their home country.
The Food and Drug Administration, which checks if medicines are safe.

questions.question: What did the Justice Department do about Jerome Powell?
Who might become the new Fed chair?
What did the court say about President Trump's asylum ban?
What is the FDA speeding up?
Who urged Senator Tillis to approve Kevin Warsh?

background_read: The Federal Reserve is like the bank for the U.S. government. It helps control how much money is available and sets interest rates, which affect how much it costs to borrow money. The chairperson is the leader of this bank. Sometimes, the government investigates people to see if they did something wrong. In this story, the investigation into Jerome Powell ended. Asylum is when a country lets people stay because they are not safe in their own country. The FDA makes sure medicines are safe before people can use them.
Article_Structure: WHO: Jerome Powell, Kevin Warsh, President Trump, the Justice Department, a federal appeals court, the FDA
WHAT: The Justice Department dropped a criminal probe into Jerome Powell, a court ruled an asylum ban illegal, and the FDA will fast-track reviews of three drugs.
WHERE: The United States, specifically Washington D.C. and the southern border.

WHY: The Justice Department ended the probe to allow Kevin Warsh to be confirmed; the court ruled because immigration law gives people the right to apply for asylum; the FDA wants to help treat mental health conditions faster.

perspectives.description: The White House wants Kevin Warsh confirmed quickly to lead the Fed.
The court believes the president cannot override immigration laws about asylum.
